publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 166, folios 218-219. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: I send you a curious letter I received this morning.--
I have been a stupid, heavy, idle lout these 2 days. Now I think it is blowing over again, & I am returning to activity. It is not that I have been ill at all.
But I have been alternately flighty , (hating to settle to anything), & stupid; I have grown rather puffy & large also.
The fact is it has been the critical time; & as I seem to be coming out of my stupidity now, without results ; I conclude that as before, it has very probably missed for this time.
But this certainly proves, (if it is so), that
things are not yet at all settled, & that time alone can prove what nature intends.--

The Hallidays called here a day or two ago, & left word they would be in Town again in a few days, to remain I believe some time. I am delighted at it. They will be delightful Theatrical companions for me, (& without my having to
pay for more than myself too, which is a consideration you know that I am not able wholly to despise).--

I do not think I am today in very remarkable spirits. It appears to me that I am too flighty, desultory, & unstable an animal; too really Bird-like , or Butterfly- like, in short; ever to accomplish anything of moment. However, time will show!--
